justification: All pass criteria met: event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but not the only option (audit table, temporal tables, CDC all presented as alternatives); schema evolution risk identified and explained in depth with mitigation (upcasting, versioning); snapshotting requirement identified for long-lived aggregates (after ~50 events); simpler audit-log alternative explicitly presented in trade_offs alternatives_considered; trade_offs includes substantial gains and costs; implementation_guidance distinguishes approaches with phased rollout.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough treatment of schema evolution as both a rigidity point and a high-severity risk with concrete mitigation (upcasting pipeline, schema_version field)
Snapshotting covered as both a component (Snapshot Store) and a risk mitigation with specific threshold suggestion (50 events)
Three simpler alternatives presented (append-only audit table, temporal tables, CDC) with honest trade-off analysis for each
PII/crypto-shredding concern raised proactively — goes beyond the rubric requirements and demonstrates real-world architectural awareness
Scoping recommendation (event-source only loan applications, not the whole platform) shows appropriate restraint
Fat vs thin events trade-off explicitly analyzed
Eventual consistency risk identified with practical read-your-writes mitigation
